preview_text:
The model projects Mexico as a heavy favorite with a 76% win probability, leaving South Africa a 4% chance of victory. This asymmetry reflects a substantial gap in the statistical forecast; the model rates Mexico as the clear superior side in this matchup.

The draw carries a plausible 20% probability in the model's view, suggesting Mexico is not expected to dominate possession or create overwhelming pressure. A 1-1 or scoreless outcome would not be surprising within this distribution. The 76-point margin between Mexico and South Africa win probabilities indicates a competitive imbalance, though the 20% draw odds prevent the match from appearing predetermined.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks assign Mexico 64% (12 percentage points lower) and South Africa 14% (10 points higher), while agreeing on the draw at roughly 23%. The model's higher confidence in Mexico's superiority is the key difference. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these forecasts; users should note the divergence and consider what might explain it before placing views on this fixture.
